titleStmt.title: SII 25.137: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 137. (A.R. No. 137 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab in the prākāra of the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This inscription is dated in the 4th year and 4635th day of his reign. This record carries the puḷḷi over the consonants.

It begins with the Sanskrit verse which records the gift of 15 kāśu by Kāḍambavēḷāṉ for the temple of Śiva at Nūtanagrāma. It is stated that a lamp was to be maintained in the above temple from out of the interest accrued out of the gift amount.

The Tamil portion following the Sanskrit verse records the same fact but in greater detail. It states that Maṟavaṉ Aṇukkap-pēraraiyaṉ alias Kaḍambaṉ-vēḷāṉ of Perumāttūr in Muttūṟṟuk-kūṟṟam made a gift of 15 paḻaṅkāśu for a perpetual lamp to be maintained to the deity Tiruttaḷibhaṭārar of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Koḻuvūrk-kūṟṟam. It is also mentioned that from out of the interest accrued out of 15 kāśu besides a perpetual lamp to the deity, a nilaiviḷakku was also to be maintained. This donor is also stated to have endowed 15 paḻaṅkāśu for the supply of garlands to this Mahādēva. The sabhai of Tirupputtūr undertook to supply 15 vaḍam daily, each vaḍam measuring a length of two muḻam, to the deity from out of the interest accrued from the gift amount. The garland is of the width of two fingers and carries two fresh flowers (naṟumpū) at each point.
A body called Āyiratteḻu-nūṟṟuvar is mentioned. The word Tirupputtūr is a Tamil form of the Sanskrit expression Nūtanagrāma. The word is split into Pudu+ūr meaning ‘nūtana’ and ‘grāma’.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 25.137 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
